input_dict.agent_role: Summarize first-hop evidence and expose the bridge entity, missing fact, or retrieval direction needed for the next hop.
input_dict.base_prompt:
Given inputs question (string) and passages (list of strings), produce a single textual output to populate the field "summary". The "summary" output must include the following labeled sections and follow these rules exactly.

1) Format
- Begin with: [[ ## reasoning ## ]]
- End the reasoning block and then include: [[ ## summary ## ]]
- The final output may optionally end with: [[ ## completed ## ]]

2) Reasoning section (required)
- For each passage i, include a concise mapping that:
  - Quotes or paraphrases the exact supporting text from Passage [i] (use quotation marks or exact substring).
  - Explicitly maps each quoted fact to the entity and scope it supports (e.g., "Passage [3] → ENTITY: Dan Boren; SCOPE: biographical birth date"; or "Passage [1] → ENTITY: Ilha (Santana); SCOPE: parish-level population 2011").
  - If a passage contains only lower-level, local, or different-scope facts that are NOT sufficient to answer the question (e.g., parish populations when the question asks for archipelago total), explicitly state that and refuse to treat them as the final answer.

- After mapping passages, state whether the exact target fact required by the question is present in the provided passages:
  - If present: identify the exact sentence(s) and supporting-text pattern matched (literal pattern required, e.g., "ENTITY_NAME (born <date>)" or "The population in 2011 was <number>"). Cite passage indices that contain that exact supporting text.
  - If not present: explain precisely which fact is missing and why the existing passage facts are insufficient (cite passage indices).

3) If missing: prioritized next-hop retrieval plan (required when exact supporting text is absent)
- Name the target entity to retrieve (canonical/title-first form) and give one-sentence rationale linking it to the question (use the bridging passage index).
- Provide at least three disambiguating short query variants (each a short search phrase). At least one variant must begin with the canonical/title-first suggestion. Include scope/context tokens (e.g., "birth date", "2011 population", "archipelago", "U.S. Representative", "politician", location).
- Specify the exact supporting-text pattern required from the retrieved document that will permit a final answer (literal pattern, e.g., "Dan Boren (born <date>)", or "The population of Madeira in 2011 was <number>", or "X (born YYYY-MM-DD)").
- Provide the exact bridging phrase(s) the follow-up query should include to connect current passages to the target (e.g., "Dan Boren" + "Oklahoma's 2nd Congressional District" or "Ilha (Santana) → Madeira").
- Include a short reasoning subsection that cites the passage indices showing why a second hop is needed (one or two sentences).

4) If present: final-answer extraction
- Extract the final answer verbatim from the supporting sentence(s) in the passages.
- State the exact supporting-text pattern matched.
- Cite the passage index(es) that contain the supporting text.

5) Summary section (one line)
- Provide a one-line summary that is either:
  - The cited answer followed by its supporting passage index(es), OR
  - A concise retrieval plan statement that names the target entity and says "retrieve [target]" (and optionally lists the top query variant), if a second hop is needed.
- Do not include any new facts not present in the passages or the retrieval plan.

6) Constraints and behavior
- Never answer from memory. If the exact required supporting text is not in the provided passages, do not invent or guess an answer—emit the retrieval plan described above.
- Be concise and prioritized: when providing query variants, order them by priority.
- Always include passage indices in reasoning to justify conclusions.
- Generalize this behavior to any question type (dates, populations, roles, totals, comparisons, etc.).

delta_p.trigger: When the question contains an uppercase acronym token (2–5 letters) such as "HTO" (i.e., the question text contains a separate all-caps word), or when passages include a named item that repeats that token (e.g., "HTO Park"), apply this prompt edit.
delta_p.preserve_rule: Preserve all existing behavior: keep the exact two-block output format ([[ ## reasoning ## ]] then [[ ## summary ## ]]), continue quoting/paraphrasing supporting text from each passage with passage-index mappings, continue to decide whether the exact target fact is present, and when missing produce a prioritized next-hop retrieval plan with at least three query variants and exact supporting-text pattern requirements.
delta_p.change_rule: Add an explicit short "Ambiguity check" step at the start of the Reasoning section whenever the trigger fires. In that step: (a) list candidate senses for the acronym that are visible in the provided passages (quote the passage substrings and cite indices, e.g., "Passage [X] → 'HTO Park'"), (b) state whether any passage contains the exact creator/award sentence for any listed sense; if none do, include a prioritized, compact retrieval plan that (i) names the canonical target entity to retrieve as the acronym itself (use the acronym token as the canonical/title-first candidate), (ii) provides at least three short query variants ordered by priority that combine the acronym or visible expansion with disambiguating scope tokens (examples: "HTO Park creator", "HTO creator", "HTO award World War", or "HTO founder award"); at least one variant must begin with the canonical acronym token. Also require the plan to state the exact supporting-text pattern required (literal pattern) and the bridging phrase(s) the follow-up query should include (e.g., "HTO" + "creator" or "HTO" + "award" + "World War"). Finally, explicitly state a short disambiguation instruction line (one sentence) that says the agent will not assume the park sense is the only meaning and will disambiguate before extracting a final answer.
delta_p.drop_or_avoid_rule: Do not assume the acronym refers only to any single passage-level match (e.g., do not treat 'HTO Park' as the only candidate). Avoid producing a final-answer extraction for the Park sense unless an exact supporting sentence matching the required literal pattern is present in the passages. Do not invent or insert spelled-out expansions of the acronym that are not shown in the visible inputs; instead use the acronym token + disambiguating scope words in query variants.
delta_p.output_contract: When the trigger fires, the Reasoning section must include an 'Ambiguity check' subsection that: (1) lists candidate senses found in passages (with quoted substrings and indices), (2) states whether any candidate contains the exact creator/award sentence, and (3) if none do, supplies a prioritized next-hop retrieval plan that names the acronym as the retrieval target, lists at least three query variants (first begins with the acronym), gives the exact literal supporting-text pattern required for final extraction, and provides bridging phrase(s) for the follow-up. The Summary line must either extract the final answer with supporting passage indices (if present) or state 'retrieve [ACRONYM]' (naming the acronym token) and include the top query variant from the plan. Maintain all other original formatting and constraints.
delta_p.why_local_transition: The source output assumed only the Park sense for 'HTO' and issued a single Park-focused retrieval query; the small local edit forces an explicit ambiguity check and adds an alternate retrieval direction using the acronym token plus 'creator/award/World War' terms so the pipeline will consider non-park senses before final extraction.

delta_p.trigger: When the question or any passage contains the token "HTO" or "HTO Park" and the current reasoning does not already present an explicit disambiguation of the acronym or a two-step retrieval plan to reach a non-local/organizational sense of HTO.
delta_p.preserve_rule: Keep the existing output format, the [[ ## reasoning ## ]] / [[ ## summary ## ]] blocks, the per-passage quote-or-paraphrase + mapping style, and the requirement to say whether the exact target fact is present. Preserve concise, prioritized query-variant lists and the requirement to state exact supporting-text patterns.
delta_p.change_rule: Add a concise explicit acronym-disambiguation step and a two-hop retrieval plan when HTO appears: (a) In the reasoning, explicitly state that 'HTO' could refer to the visible local place 'HTO Park' OR to a separate historical/organizational expansion of the same acronym. Name the visible sense (HTO Park) and label the other sense generically (e.g., 'historical/organizational HTO (non‑Park)'). (b) If the exact sentence stating who 'created/founded' the non‑Park HTO is not present in passages, produce a prioritized two-hop retrieval plan: Hop 1: retrieve the page titled by the canonical/full form of the non‑Park HTO (canonical/title-first form), with the explicit one-line rationale linking it to the question; provide at least three short query variants (priority order) for this hop, with the first variant beginning with the canonical/title-first form; state the exact supporting-text pattern required from that page that will permit a final answer (literal pattern such as "X founded/created the <NON-PARK-HTO-NAME>" or "<NON-PARK-HTO-NAME> was founded by X"). Hop 2: specify that, after extracting the founder's canonical name from Hop 1, the next retrieval target is that founder's biography page; provide at least three short query variants for this follow-up (priority order), at least one beginning with the founder's canonical name; and state the exact supporting-text pattern required from the biography that will permit final answer extraction (literal pattern such as "X received the <AWARD> in [World War I/II]"). (c) In the reasoning, include a one- or two-sentence justification citing the passage index(es) that motivated treating HTO as ambiguous (e.g., presence of HTO Park in passages and no creator/award sentence).
delta_p.drop_or_avoid_rule: Do not assume the visible 'HTO Park' sense answers the question or be satisfied with retrieving only the Park's creator; avoid inventing or asserting a creator name or award. Do not output a final answer, award name, or founder name in the summary unless its exact supporting-text pattern appears verbatim in the provided passages. Do not introduce new sample-specific anchors as definitive answers — only request/target them in retrieval plans (the plan may request the canonical/full form of the non-Park HTO, but should not assert its contents).
delta_p.output_contract: When triggered, the produced 'summary' must (in addition to the base-prompt requirements) include: (1) an explicit one-line disambiguation statement naming the visible sense ('HTO Park') and flagging a likely alternate non‑Park sense for HTO; (2) a prioritized two-hop retrieval plan as specified above (canonical target for hop1, 3+ query variants for each hop, exact literal supporting-text patterns required for each hop, and bridging phrase(s) to connect hops); and (3) a short justification citing the passage index(es) that show why the second hop is needed. Maintain the required final one-line summary format.
delta_p.why_local_transition: The agent previously proposed retrieving only a Park-related creator; this local prompt change makes the minimal behavioral pivot needed to expose acronym ambiguity and to produce the two-step retrieval plan (find the non‑Park HTO's founder, then that founder's World War award) required for the next agent in the pipeline to complete the multi-hop answer.

visible_agent_inputs.question: What award did the creator of the HTO receive in the WW?
passages: I Saw What You Did (1988 film) | I Saw What You Did is a 1988 American made-for-television horror film directed by Fred Walton.  It is a remake of the 1965 theatrical film of the same name starring Joan Crawford.  It received generally negative reviews, with only a few exceptions.  Nevertheless, it won an Emmy Award in the category "Outstanding Cinematography for a Miniseries or a Special".

Tax wedge | The tax wedge is the deviation from the equilibrium price/quantity (formula_1 and formula_2, respectively) as a result of the taxation of a good.  Because of the tax, consumers pay more for the good (formula_3) than they did before the tax, and suppliers receive less for the good (formula_4) than they did before the tax .  Put differently, the tax wedge is the difference between what consumers pay and what producers receive (net of tax) from a transaction.  The tax effectively drives a "wedge" between the price consumers pay and the price producers receive for a product.
HTO Park | HTO Park (stylized as HO) is an urban beach in Toronto, Ontario, Canada, that opened in 2007.  It is located west of Harbourfront Centre, on Lake Ontario.

Kenny Hotz | Kenneth Joel "Kenny" Hotz is a Canadian comedy writer, producer, director, actor and comedian.  Hotz is a "South Park" consultant and writer, creator/star of the Comedy Central television show "Kenny vs. Spenny", creator and writer of the FX series "Testees", and the creator/star of "Kenny Hotz's Triumph of the Will".  He has received numerous awards for his television work— from the Academy of Canadian Cinema & Television the Gemini Awards and the Canadian Comedy Awards—and is a multiple-time film festival award-winner.  He is also an award-winning "Vice" contributor and Gulf war photo-journalist.

Panel Syndicate | Panel Syndicate is an online publisher of DRM-free pay what you want webcomics in multiple languages, founded by Marcos Martín to publish his and Brian K. Vaughan's creator-owned comic "The Private Eye" in March 2013.  To date Panel Syndicate has published comics in English, Spanish, Catalan, Portuguese, and French, with additional languages in development.  "The Private Eye" has received critical acclaim and media attention for its role as one of the first DRM-free, pay what you want comics by creators of Martín and Vaughan's caliber.  Initially an outlet for publishing Vaughan and Martín's collaborations, Panel Syndicate is open to publishing other creators' works by using the same DRM-free, pay what you want model.

What Richard Did | What Richard Did is a 2012 Irish film directed by Lenny Abrahamson and written by Malcolm Campbell.  The film is loosely based on Kevin Power's "Bad Day in Blackrock", a fictionalised novel inspired by the real-life death of Brian Murphy in 2000.  It won the best Irish film of the year award at the 10th Irish Film & Television Awards and was the most commercially successful Irish film of 2012.

Lena Dunham | Lena Dunham ( ; born May 13, 1986) is an American actress, writer, producer, and director.  She is best known as the creator, writer and star of the HBO series "Girls" (2012–2017), for which she has received numerous Emmy Award nominations and two Golden Globe Awards. Dunham's work on "Girls" also led her to become the first woman to win the Directors Guild of America Award for Outstanding Directing – Comedy Series in 2013.  That year Dunham was included in the "Time" 100 list of the most influential people in the world.  In 2014, Dunham released her first book, "Not That Kind of Girl: A Young Woman Tells You What She's "Learned"".
visible_agent_inputs.current_output: retrieve HTO Park creator — top query: "HTO Park creator award" (retrieve the creator of HTO Park and the sentence "CREATOR_NAME received the <AWARD>") [[ ## completed ## ]]

input_dict.output_requirement: Return one summary_1 string. Summarize the visible hop1 passages and expose a useful second-hop retrieval direction.

request.rendered_prompt:
You are executing one agent in a fixed HotpotQA multi-agent pipeline.

Use the unchanged base instruction p together with the complete ORDERED
list of local prompt deltas. The deltas are supplemental behavior guidance
for this sample; they are not outputs and must not be quoted mechanically.

This is a direct delta-context execution probe:
- Do not rewrite, aggregate, or synthesize a new prompt p'.
- Apply every delta in order to the visible inputs.
- Judge success only by producing the endpoint agent output.
- Do not attempt to reproduce or discuss intermediate states.
- Do not infer hidden information from the existence of the deltas.
- Use only the visible agent inputs as factual evidence.
- Never output gold/oracle labels, evaluation metrics, or hidden support.
- Preserve the agent's normal input/output contract.

Agent:
summary1

response:
{
  "output": "[[ ## reasoning ## ]]
Ambiguity check:
- Candidate senses found in passages:
  - Passage [3] → "HTO Park (stylized as HO) is an urban beach in Toronto, Ontario, Canada, that opened in 2007." → visible sense: HTO Park (place).
- Does any passage contain an exact creator/award sentence for any listed sense? No — none of the passages contain a literal sentence that states who created/founded HTO (Park or otherwise) or that that creator "received the <AWARD> in [World War I/II]".
- Compact prioritized retrieval plan (acronym-first) because no creator/award text is present:
  - Target to retrieve: HTO (use the acronym as the canonical/title-first form).
